title: Loading image archives created by `podman save` does not make `podman pull` noop
body:
### Issue Description

If I `podman pull x` and then `podman save x`, I would expect that if I ran `podman load x` on an identical system, that other system would not download any blobs when running `podman pull x` (in cases where the manifests are the same).

### Steps to reproduce the issue

I am reproducing this with podman in podman for ease of illustration. I create a ubi9 container with podman 5.4.0 like this which I'll use to create two "environments"
```Dockerfile
FROM registry.access.redhat.com/ubi9/ubi:9.5-1739751568
RUN dnf -yq install https://rpmfind.net/linux/centos-stream/9-stream/AppStream/x86_64/os/Packages/podman-5.4.0-1.el9.x86_64.rpm
ENTRYPOINT bash
```

I run that env with a volume mounted in to save the image archive
```bash
# start a fresh env, pull and save the image
podman run -it --rm -v /root/images:/root/images mycontainer
podman pull mirror.gcr.io/library/node:23-alpine3.20
podman save -o /root/images/nodealpine mirror.gcr.io/library/node:23-alpine3.20
exit
# start a second env, load the container and then try pulling
podman run -it --rm -v /root/images:/root/images mycontainer
podman load -i /root/images/nodealpine
podman pull mirror.gcr.io/library/node:23-alpine3.20 # <- this downloads blobs
```

I am confused why this happens since a `podman manifest inspect mirror.gcr.io/library/node:23-alpine3.20` in the first container instance (before the save) is identical to the manifest in the second container instance (after the load, before the pull)

Curiously, if you [pull, save, delete, load, pull] within one podman instance, the blobs do not get re-downloaded
```bash
# start a fresh env, pull and save the image
podman run -it --rm -v /root/images:/root/images mycontainer
podman pull mirror.gcr.io/library/node:23-alpine3.20
podman save -o /root/images/nodealpine mirror.gcr.io/library/node:23-alpine3.20
# delete the image from podman, then reload it from archive
podman image rm mirror.gcr.io/library/node:23-alpine3.20
podman load -i /root/images/nodealpine
podman pull mirror.gcr.io/library/node:23-alpine3.20 # <- no blobs redownloaded
```

So it seems like the original instance of podman holds onto something that lets _it_ noop subsequent pulls, but that state does not survive the `podman save` process. Is this just a limitation of the docker-archive/oci-archive formats?

### Describe the results you received

Pulls after loading an image into an identical environment does not prevent `podman pull` from fetching blobs

### Describe the results you expected

A loaded image on an identical system should not need to fetch any blobs

### podman info output

### Additional information

comments.body:
I am very sure this is expected, generally podman save creates an entirely new image from the uncompressed data (maybe even in a different format compared to the registry). You simply cannot assume that this process will ever result in the same manifest data ,e.g. https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/18809

If you want reuse stick to registries and don't copy the image archives around.

Leaving it for @mtrmac to confirm.

Yes; don’t use whole-image archives, _especially_ if your goal is to minimize data transfers by reuse: whole-image archives are either uncompressed (wasting space) or don’t allow random access (forcing `podman load` to decompress the whole thing first). Also, the archive formats are lossy, constraining what can be done at all.

If at all possible, use a real registry; even if it had to be a short-term registry running only for the purpose of populating the local storage. Or, I guess, Additional Image Stores, distributing pre-pulled uncompressed files, but that’s harder to manage especially as images change.

---

> I am confused why this happens since a `podman manifest inspect mirror.gcr.io/library/node:23-alpine3.20` in the first container instance (before the save) is identical

I suspect (but I didn’t verify) that this is a fallout of how `podman manifest inspect` silently/transparently/sometimes reads from the registry if the name does not exist locally.

When that `docker-archive` does not save manifests at all, and `oci-archive` would have to update the manifest to refer to either uncompressed or re-compressed layers, I think it’s rather unlikely that `podman load` could reproduce exactly the on-registry manifest.

---

The technical explanation is that Podman deduplicates images by (config digest, uncompressed layer identities); and when pulling a compressed image, the only way to _securely_ determine uncompressed layer identities is to pull + decompress. So, the presence of already-uncompressed layers as created by `podman load` does not help.

But there _is_ a record of known-correct compressed/uncompressed layer correspondences, so after a first pull, the code can determine that the layers exist locally and avoid a second pull. That’s why this works when everything happens on one computer.
Well I tried to move this to a discussion as I thought it might be good to get some more visibility there but github seems to be broken and is unable to move the issue so I just close it
